acceptance_targets: [ch13_craft_001] PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:29 no longer anchors the chapter to September and all explicit time cues in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:350 remain consistent with a late-fall placement; FAIL if September remains or conflicting seasonal markers persist.

[ch13_craft_002] PASS if the revised confession span (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:171-177 equivalent) includes at least two concrete, non-euphemistic consequences tied to Gideon disbursements and Lena's response at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:193 names one specific harmed party or material loss; FAIL if the beat can still be summarized as abstract institutional guilt without concrete fallout.

[ch13_booker_001] PASS if the revised second scene (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:281-292 equivalent) produces one irreversible new decision not already present after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:45-82, and the core refusal rationale is not restated in the same terms twice; FAIL if scene two mainly replays scene one's denial logic.

[FBR-02-001] Pass if the chapter_13 scene no longer names September and now reads as late fall consistent with the dated October 26, 2026 events in chapter_17; fail if any explicit month/season cue in chapter_13 still places it in September/early-fall.
[FBR-02-012] Pass if both cited lines no longer use the '... like ...' impact template and chapter_13's tension reads through concrete corridor/operational detail; fail if either template remains.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:29 no longer anchors the chapter to September and all explicit time cues in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:350 remain consistent with a late-fall placement; FAIL if September remains or conflicting seasonal markers persist.
findings.problem: The chapter explicitly tags the scene as September, but canon places chapter_13 inside the late-fall run (with chapter_17 fixed at October 26, 2026). This creates timeline drift against established continuity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the temporal marker at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:29 so the weather/month cue aligns with late-fall continuity (or remove explicit month naming). Recheck downstream temporal cues at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:350 for consistency.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised confession span (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:171-177 equivalent) includes at least two concrete, non-euphemistic consequences tied to Gideon disbursements and Lena's response at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:193 names one specific harmed party or material loss; FAIL if the beat can still be summarized as abstract institutional guilt without concrete fallout.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift in the confession beat: Eleanor's admission stays inside legal-financial abstractions ('endowment growth,' 'campaign debt,' 'title questions') at the exact moment the narrative needs concrete, embodied consequence. The euphemistic frame softens moral exposure instead of forcing it into material terms.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the confession exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:171 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:175, plus Lena's processing beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:193, so at least one concrete dispossession consequence is named on-page (who lost what, what was funded, and what social cost was externalized). Keep Eleanor defensive, but do not let abstraction be the only vocabulary.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised second scene (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:281-292 equivalent) produces one irreversible new decision not already present after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:45-82, and the core refusal rationale is not restated in the same terms twice; FAIL if scene two mainly replays scene one's denial logic.
findings.problem: The chapter runs two near-parallel refusal loops (annex cancellation denial, then corridor contract denial) with similar rhetorical shape. Repetition lowers escalation pressure in a chapter that must sustain dread through consequence, not re-argued position.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the second confrontation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:281-292 from the first refusal cycle at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:45-82 by introducing a new irreversible turn (e.g., explicit written override, named legal exposure, or concrete operational concession that changes tomorrow's risk map).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the chapter_13 scene no longer names September and now reads as late fall consistent with the dated October 26, 2026 events in chapter_17; fail if any explicit month/season cue in chapter_13 still places it in September/early-fall.
findings.problem: Timeline contradiction: chapter_13 frames the gala-prep day as September, but cycle continuity fixes chapter_17 at October 26, 2026 and places chapters 13-18 in late fall.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise time/season cues at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4472 and align adjacent environmental markers at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4446, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4590, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4802 so chapter_13 clearly reads as late-fall timing immediately preceding the late-arc events.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines no longer use the '... like ...' impact template and chapter_13's tension reads through concrete corridor/operational detail; fail if either template remains.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book formula metaphor pattern appears across 6 chapters; this chapter repeats it multiple times (e.g., lines 4580 and 4678), compounding the tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace template lines at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4580 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4678 with differentiated sensory-pressure language tied to location logistics and tactical stakes.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
